compute: Cloud Run container instances have configurable CPU, memory, and concurrency.
Request timeout and max instance settings should match workload behavior.
Use service accounts with least privilege.
functions: Cloud Functions generation and runtime choice affects timeout and scaling limits.
Event trigger availability depends on region.
Use Cloud Run functions for newer event-driven workloads.
database: Cloud SQL machine type, storage, and HA availability vary by engine and region.
Private IP and authorized networks should be configured before cutover.
Automated backups and point-in-time recovery should be reviewed.
storage: Cloud Storage objects support lifecycle policies and retention controls.
Bucket location type affects latency, availability, and cost.
